title.en: I messed up! I shouldn't have done that! 아, 완전 망했다!
title.ko: 아, 완전 망했다! 그러지 말걸 그랬어!

description.en: When you make an unexpected mistake or regret the past! Practice expressing your honest regret with 완전 망했다 and 그러지 말걸 그랬어.
description.ko: 예상치 못한 실수를 저질러 자책하거나 지난 일을 후회하고 싶을 때! 완전 망했다와 그러지 말걸 그랬어를 사용해 솔직한 아쉬움을 표현해 봅니다.

tags: Reaction. Regret. Mistake. Self-blame. Friendship. Daily Life

content.speaker: John
content.text: What's up world! Welcome back. You are listening to... "Real Reaction!" I'm your host. John.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Mina. I think I'm in big trouble. I just realized I accidentally deleted our next week's script file.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 네? 정말요? 백업 파일도 안 만들어 놨단 말이에요?
content.translation: What? John. really? You mean you didn't even make a backup file?

content.speaker: John
content.text: I forgot! 완전 망했네요.
content.translation: I forgot! Oh. I completely messed up.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 미리 복사해둘걸 그랬어요. 제 손가락이 왜 그랬을까요?
content.translation: I should have copied it in advance. Why did my fingers do that?

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 이미 일어난 일인데 어쩌겠어요. 제가 같이 다시 써줄게요!
content.translation: It already happened. so what can we do? I'll help you rewrite it together!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 완전 망했다. This sounds very dramatic. right.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 네. 어떤 일이 아주 잘못되었거나 실패했을 때 쓰는 강한 표현이에요.
content.translation: Yes. It's a strong expression used when something has gone very wrong or failed.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 친구들 사이에서는 가벼운 실수에도 "망했다!" 라고 장난스럽게 쓰기도 하죠.
content.translation: Among friends. we even use "I messed up!" jokingly for minor mistakes.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 완전 망했다.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Then what about 그러지 말걸 그랬어.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 이건 과거의 행동에 대해 후회할 때 쓰는 가장 대표적인 말이에요.
content.translation: This is the most representative phrase used when regretting past actions.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: "할걸 그랬어" 는 해야 했는데 안 해서 아쉽다는 뜻이고. "하지 말걸 그랬어" 는 해서 후회한다는 뜻이죠.
content.translation: "~Hal-geol geu-raet-eo" means you regret not doing something you should have. and "~ha-ji mal-geol geu-raet-eo" means you regret doing something.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 그러지 말걸 그랬어.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Okay. let's try another situation. Imagine I bought an incredibly expensive suit that I don't even need.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 존. 방금 배달 온 그 옷 뭐예요? 평소에 입기에는 너무 화려한 것 같은데요?
content.translation: John. what is that clothes that just got delivered? It looks too flashy to wear normally.

content.speaker: John
content.text: I know. I was just blinded by the sale and bought it impulsively.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 가격이 꽤 비싸 보이는데. 환불 가능한가요?
content.translation: It looks quite expensive. Is it refundable?

content.speaker: John
content.text: No. it was a final sale. 아 완전 망했어요.
content.translation: No. it was a final sale. Oh. I'm totally ruined.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 그냥 사지 말걸 그랬어요. 이 돈이면 맛있는 걸 몇 번이나 먹을 수 있는데!
content.translation: I just shouldn't have bought it. With this money. I could have eaten delicious food so many times!

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 이미 산 거니까 어쩔 수 없죠. 다음 파티 때 입고 주인공이 되어보세요!
content.translation: Since you already bought it. there's nothing you can do. Wear it to the next party and be the star!

content.speaker: John
content.text: Everyone makes mistakes. The important thing is to move on.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 맞아요. 후회는 짧게 하고 그 실수를 통해 배우는 게 더 중요해요.
content.translation: Right. It's more important to keep the regret short and learn through that mistake.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 오늘 배운 표현들로 여러분의 솔직한 감정을 털어내고 다시 기운 내시길 바랄게요!
content.translation: I hope you shake off your honest feelings with the expressions we learned today and cheer up again!

content.speaker: John
content.text: Let's review our "regret" reactions one last time.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 완전 망했다! I messed up!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 그러지 말걸 그랬어! I shouldn't have done that!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 내 실수야! It's my mistake!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 자책하지 마! Don't blame yourself!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 후회해도 소용없어! It's no use regretting it!

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 여러분도 오늘 혹시 실수했다면 너무 속상해하지 말고 "괜찮아"라고 스스로를 다독여 주세요.
content.translation: If you made a mistake today. don't be too upset and comfort yourself by saying "It's okay."

content.speaker: John
content.text: That's all for today. See you in the next episode.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 다음에 또 만나요. 안녕~!
content.translation: See you again next time. Bye~!

vocabulary.meaning: To be ruined. To mess up
vocabulary.example: 이번 시험은 공부를 안 해서 완전히 망했어요. (I completely messed up this exam because I didn't study.)
선택에 후회가 없도록 신중하게 결정하세요. (Decide carefully so there are no regrets in your choice.)
너무 자책하지 마세요. 누구나 실수할 수 있어요. (Don't blame yourself too much. Anyone can make a mistake.)
충동적으로 물건을 사면 나중에 후회하기 쉬워요. (If you buy things impulsively. it's easy to regret it later.)
제 실수로 파일이 지워져서 정말 죄송합니다. (I'm very sorry that the file was deleted due to my mistake.)
슬퍼하는 친구의 마음을 따뜻하게 다독여 주었어요. (I warmly comforted my sad friend's heart.)
